'''Logan Campbell''' (born 23 May 1971) is a former [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland international]] [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a professional in Australia and England. He also represented [[Auckland rugby league team|Auckland]].

==Background==
He was born in New Zealand.<ref name=deadline>[https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-ruane-in-as-widnes-ring-the-changes-transfers-deadline-1399366.html Ruane in as Widnes ring the changes: Transfers deadline ] ''The Independent'', 11 January 1994</ref>

==Early years==
A [[Northcote Tigers]] junior, in 1991 Campbell won the [[Auckland Rugby League club trophies|Tetley Trophy]], topping the try scoring charts in [[Auckland Rugby League]]'s Fox Memorial Trophy. During this time Campbell also represented [[Auckland rugby league team|Auckland]].<ref>[http://www.aucklandleague.co.nz/display_page.asp?item=373 Northcote and Birkenhead Tigers Rugby League and Sports Club Inc Historical Information] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110720071934/http://www.aucklandleague.co.nz/display_page.asp?item=373 |date=20 July 2011}} ''Auckland Rugby League'', 2009</ref>

==Playing career==
Campbell played for the [[Newcastle Knights]] in 1993. Even though he only played one game for the [[Newcastle Knights]], he is famous with older fans for debuting on the bench with the then 19-year-old [[Andrew Johns]].{{Citation needed|date=October 2010}}

In early 1994 he joined the [[London Broncos#1991–1994: London Crusaders|London Crusaders]]. In 1996 he joined [[Workington Town]] in the new [[Super League]] competition. He joined [[Hull FC]] in 1997 for three seasons before spending 2000 with the [[Castleford Tigers]]. In 2001 he returned to Hull for another season before being released.<ref>[http://www.thefreelibrary.com/Rugby+League%3A+Ins+and+outs+for+Super+League+2002-a083370060 Ins and outs for Super League 2002] ''The Racing Post'', 1 March 2002</ref>

==Representative career==
Campbell played two matches for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] in 1998. He was selected for the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup|2000 World Cup]] for Scotland but never played in a match.<ref>[http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/933736.stm Aussie is a World Cup Scot] BBC Sport, 20 September 2000</ref>
